rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 22, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANTS MAERSK, INC., AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC. AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S MOTION To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 22, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANTS MAERSK, INC., AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC. AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT REFLECT THE HOLDINGS OF HOWLETT V BIRKDALE SHIPPING CO. (1994) 512 U.S. 92, 98 WHICH DEFINES THE CIRCUMSTANCES UNDER WHICH A SHORE BASED WORKER CAN BRING SUIT. THE COMPLAINT IS VAGUE, AMBIGUOUS AND UNCERTAIN AS TO JUST WHAT PLAINTIFF IS CLAIMING IN THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THE VESSELS WERE UNFIT FOR THEIR INTENDED PURPOSE, AND IN THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DISTINGUISH BETWEEN SHORE BASED WORKERS AND SEAMAN. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

THE HAMILTON DECLARATION DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, A VS. U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, A DELAWARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 22, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE RESPECTIVE POLICIES PROVIDE, SUBSTANTIVELY, THE SAME COVERAGE. EACH COVERS SUDDEN AND ACCIDENTAL DISCHARGES. DEFENDANT IS NOT OBLIGATED TO DROP DOWN. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO RELY UPON EXHAUSTION, IT MUST FILE A NEW ACTION AND/OR SEEK TO AMEND THIS COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RACE C JONES VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 22, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, MICHAEL PEEVEY, FRED HARRIS, NANCY RODRIGUEZ AND BECKY REYES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH FOUR WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT IS THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM: FILARSKY V SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 28 CAL. 4TH 419, 433. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS FAILED TO FILE A TORT CLAIM. OVERRULED AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT AUTHORNIZES INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES FOR VIOLATIONS OF THE ACT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JULIAN P LAGOS et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF APPEALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 22, 2008 LINE 17. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. THE STANDARD OF REVIEW IS SET FORTH IN C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5. THE DEFENDANTS DID NOT ACT IN EXCESS OF THEIR JURISDICATION; THERE WAS A FAIR HEARING, AND THERE WAS NO ABUSE OF DISCRETION. COMMISSIONER GARCIA HAD NO OBLIGATION TO RECUSE HIMSELF. =(302/PJM/AY)
